channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Ex-NBA players Malik Beasley, Ed Davis indicted in gambling case
item.description: Beasley, Davis and an NBA agent are among six defendants indicted on charges related to alleged sports betting plot.

item.title: Afghan families mourn loved ones as border tensions with Pakistan rise
item.description: Afghan officials say Pakistani attacks killed at least 36 civilians, while Pakistan says its forces killed 29 fighters.

item.title: US petrol prices ease despite ongoing uncertainties with Iran
item.description: US–Iran peace talks ease market volatility, but uncertainty keeps oil prices hovering, complicating recovery efforts.

item.title: US Supreme Court rebuffs Trump’s appeal in E Jean Carroll case
item.description: The US Supreme Court has rejected a push by President Trump to throw out a jury's findings of sexual assault.

item.title: Iraq’s corruption crackdown is a start, not a solution
item.description: New PM Ali al-Zaidi's arrest of 47 officials is welcome, but the rot runs through the system that put him in power.

item.title: Gaza high school student’s fight for a future
item.description: Amid Israel’s genocidal war on Gaza that destroyed most schools, 18-year-old Dana Shabat sits her final exams online.

item.title: Fire and floods hit Ghana’s capital as residents are left stranded
item.description: A massive fire engulfed a rubber factory in Ghana’s capital, Accra, as severe flooding swamped parts of the city.

item.title: The US-Iran MoU looks at managing the pain rather than ending the war
item.description: The US-Iran MoU acts as a state-to-state framework managing mutual pain, not a definitive end to regional conflict.

item.title: Earthquake jolts southwest China
item.description: An earthquake with a magnitude of 5.5 struck Yibin in China’s southwest province of Sichuan shortly on Monday.

item.title: Lebanon Latest: Israel strikes as Hezbollah condemns new deal
item.description: Israeli strikes continue as the new Israel-Lebanon agreement faces opposition.

item.title: Lebanon’s displaced begin to return home, but some have nowhere to go
item.description: Thousands in Lebanon attempt to return home, yet rampant destruction leaves many trapped in displacement centres.

item.title: JD Vance’s 2028 strategy is starting to take shape
item.description: The vice president’s decision to criticise Israel and distance himself from rival Rubio means he is reading the base.

item.title: Five killed in shooting at German youth centre
item.description: Five people were killed in a shooting at a youth welfare centre in Stade, northern Germany.

item.title: Enzo Maresca appointed Manchester City manager to succeed Pep Guardiola
item.description: Former Blues boss signs three-year City deal to succeed Pep Guardiola who stepped down in May.

item.title: Why the Arab League could not stop Israel’s genocide
item.description: Arab governments are constrained by dependency, regime survival and fear of empowering Iran-aligned rivals.

item.title: Sudan’s el-Obeid: Life on the edge of collapse
item.description: Sudan's el-Obeid faces mass atrocities, like el-Fasher in 2025, the UN warns.

item.title: Trump announces meeting with Iran in Qatar despite military skirmishes
item.description: US president says talks will take place on Tuesday, but Tehran has not confirmed the negotiations in Doha.

item.title: Brazil vs Japan LIVE: FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Follow updates as Brazil face Japan in the last 32, as we bring you the build-up, analysis and text commentary.

item.title: Is the Israeli military deliberately targeting children in Gaza?
item.description: A UN inquiry found that children in Gaza are being shot with a single bullet.

item.title: Russian missile and drone attacks kill at least eight in Ukraine
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy condemns the 'horrific attacks' and urges allies to boost Ukraine's air defences.

item.title: Why Pakistan’s Afghan air strikes aren’t stopping armed attacks
item.description: Analysts warn military pressure alone cannot contain a threat from armed groups that has reached Pakistan's cities.

item.title: More than 1,300 deaths in Europe amid heatwave: What can countries do?
item.description: Temperatures reached 40 degrees Celsius (104 degrees Fahrenheit) in parts of Europe on Sunday.

item.title: Brazil supporters in Indonesia grab Israeli flag off motorcyclist
item.description: A video of an Israeli flag being grabbed off two young men in Indonesia has gone viral.

item.title: Five killed in shooting at youth welfare centre in Germany’s Stade
item.description: Police say several others were injured and the suspected attacker has been arrested.

item.title: Sudan says China has waived $50m loan: What’s in it for Khartoum, Beijing?
item.description: China and Sudan signed off on a waiver of $50m as Sudan’s military-led government seeks support amid Western sanctions.
